methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $800.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 22 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
SpaceX is a Series G frontier-tech company in Space & Aerospace that has raised $10B+ and carries a last post-money valuation of $800B. The addressable comp universe includes 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, and Planet Labs, though none occupy an equivalent stage or valuation tier.

Of three valuation methods tested, only stage-progression proved applicable. This method applies a typical 1.25x step-up multiplier from Series G to the next funding stage, yielding $1.0T. Peer-median analysis was inapplicable due to insufficient comparable data points at the same stage and valuation tier within Space & Aerospace; while 22 sector peers exist, fewer than three meet narrow filter criteria. Public-comp anchoring could not be executed because target revenue is not disclosed—a common constraint for private companies—though the sector median P/S across 6 public comparables stands at 64.2x.

The triangulated valuation range is $850B to $1.1T, with a median of $1.0T. This range reflects material uncertainty around the next funding round's structure and investor sentiment, given the paucity of truly comparable transactions. The analysis is heavily dependent on the stage-progression method's applicability; validation would require either comparable private funding events or disclosed revenue for public-comp anchoring.
